docs.title: Contoso IT Knowledge
docs.text:
The single source of truth for enterprise IT services, runbooks, architecture, and governance. Built for employees, agents, and platform owners.

Audience All staff · IT · Partners Classification Internal Model ITSM · Service catalog · Architecture

docs.title: Application portfolio
docs.text: Applications are tracked with a lightweight portfolio model so support, security, and finance share a common inventory.

docs.title: Tiers
docs.text: Tier Meaning Example controls T0 Business critical, regulated 24×7 support path, strict change, DR tested T1 Important productivity Standard SLA, quarterly review T2 Departmental Best effort, owner-operated T3 Shadow IT / evaluate Block or onboard

docs.title: Attributes captured
docs.text:
• Business owner and technical owner
  • Data classification
  • Identity integration (SSO or local)
  • Hosting (SaaS / Azure / on-prem)
  • Dependency services

docs.title: Support implication
docs.text: When a ticket names an application, agents check tier + owner to choose severity and escalation path.

docs.title: Labels
docs.text: Label Description Examples Public Approved for external release Marketing site copy Internal Default for staff This knowledge base Confidential Limited business need-to-know Customer lists, unreleased financials Restricted Highest sensitivity Secrets, regulated personal data

docs.title: Handling rules (summary)
docs.text: Control Internal Confidential Restricted Corporate device Required Required Required + extra monitoring External email Allowed Encryption / DLP Prohibited without Legal Sharing links Org-wide OK Specific people Named individuals only Retention Standard Extended Legal hold capable

docs.title: Enterprise architecture overview
docs.text: Contoso’s technology estate is organized into domains with clear boundaries and owners. This knowledge base documents the operating view used by support and engineering—not every detailed design pack.

docs.title: Capability map
docs.text: Domain Capability examples Identity Directory, MFA, PAM, conditional access Workplace Endpoints, M365, print, AV Network Campus, WAN, VPN, ZTNA Cloud Azure landing zones, shared platform services Data Classification, retention, analytics platforms Security SOC, CSIRT, vulnerability management Business apps ERP, CRM, custom LOB

docs.title: Context diagram
docs.text:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Users
    E[Employees]
    C[Contractors]
  end
  subgraph Edge
    Z[ZTNA / VPN]
    W[Secure web gateway]
  end
  subgraph Core
    I[Identity]
    M[M365]
    A[Azure landing zones]
    D[Data platforms]
  end
  E --> Z
  C --> Z
  Z --> I
  W --> I
  I --> M
  I --> A
  A --> D

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
1. Identity-first — every access decision starts with strong authentication.
  2. Least privilege — standing admin is exceptional; prefer just-in-time.
  3. Segment by sensitivity — data classification drives controls.
  4. Observe everything — central logging for security and reliability.
  5. Document the path — catalog + runbooks + architecture stay aligned.

docs.title: Components
docs.text: Layer Technology (sample) Purpose Directory Microsoft Entra ID Workforce identities AuthN MFA + Conditional Access Risk-based access AuthZ Groups, app roles, PIM Least privilege PAM Privileged Identity Management Just-in-time elevation HR join Workday → identity connector Joiner/mover/leaver

docs.title: Trust flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  actor User
  participant Device
  participant CA as Conditional Access
  participant IdP as Entra ID
  participant App
  User->>Device: Sign in
  Device->>IdP: Auth request
  IdP->>CA: Evaluate policy
  CA-->>IdP: Grant / MFA / Block
  IdP-->>App: Token
  App-->>User: Session

docs.title: Support boundaries
docs.text: Issue First line Escalate to Password / lockout Service Desk Identity Ops MFA device loss Service Desk Identity Ops Conditional Access block Service Desk (collect Correlation ID) Identity Engineering App SSO broken Tier 2 Messaging/App Identity + App owner

docs.title: Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Use Campus access Wired 802.1X, corporate Wi-Fi Remote user Split-tunnel VPN + ZTNA for published apps Cloud Hub-and-spoke VNets, private endpoints for data Internet egress Secure web gateway / firewall clusters

docs.title: Logical view
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  U[User / Device] --> E[Edge / VPN / ZTNA]
  E --> C[Campus / DC]
  E --> H[Azure Hub]
  H --> S1[Spoke prod]
  H --> S2[Spoke nonprod]
  C --> H

docs.title: Operational notes
docs.text:
• VPN is not required for pure SaaS M365 if traffic is optimized.
  • Legacy file shares still require VPN or published access.
  • Changes to edge paths follow normal change management.

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text: Contoso IT Knowledge is the authoritative internal library for technology services. It is organized the way modern enterprise IT operates: catalog → runbooks → architecture → governance → operations.

docs.title: How support works
docs.text:
1. Search this knowledge base for a self-service answer.
  2. If needed, open a ticket in the service portal.
  3. Agents follow runbooks and escalate using the operations matrices.
Channel Use when Knowledge base (this site) How-to, policy, architecture questions Service portal Incidents and service requests Phone Confirmed Sev 1 / major outage Security hotline Suspected compromise or data exposure

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: Channel Detail Portal https://support.contoso.example Email servicedesk@contoso.example Phone +1 (555) 010-2000 Hours Mon–Fri 08:00–18:00 local · Sev 1 24×7

docs.text:
Section Purpose Audience Service catalog What you can request and consume Everyone Runbooks How to fix or fulfill something Agents + power users Architecture How systems fit together Engineers + architects Governance Rules, SLAs, ownership Managers + all IT Operations Live response patterns Duty managers + Tier ⅔

This model mirrors common enterprise patterns: service catalog, knowledge/runbooks, reference architecture, and operating model.

docs.title: Article types
docs.text: Type Traits Catalog entry Outcomes, eligibility, request path, owner Runbook Numbered steps, verify identity, escalate when Architecture Diagrams, boundaries, trust zones Policy Scope, rules, exceptions, review cycle

docs.title: Search tips
docs.text:
• Prefer task words: reset MFA, shared mailbox, Sev 1
  • Use service names: VPN, Intune, Entra
  • Press / anywhere to open search

docs.title: Compliance overview
docs.text: Contoso IT designs services to support common control themes. This page is an operational summary, not legal advice.

docs.title: Control themes
docs.text: Theme What support staff should know Access control Verify identity before resets; no shared passwords Audit logging Prefer admin tools that log; record ticket evidence Data protection Respect classification labels on mail and files Change control Production changes need change records Vendor management Only approved subprocessors / SaaS

docs.title: Evidence habits
docs.text:
• Link the KB article used in the ticket
  • Capture correlation IDs for auth failures
  • Never paste secrets into tickets or chat

docs.title: Related policies
docs.text:
• Change management
  • Data classification
  • Security incident

docs.title: Service ownership
docs.text: Every catalog service has named accountability so escalation is predictable.

docs.title: RACI (simplified)
docs.text:
Activity Service Desk Platform Ops Service Owner Security Tier 1 fulfillment R C I I Sev 1 technical fix C R A C Catalog definition C C A/R C Security exception I C C A/R

R = Responsible · A = Accountable · C = Consulted · I = Informed

docs.title: Directory (sample)
docs.text: Service Owner Ops contact Identity Director, Identity identity-ops@contoso.example Messaging Director, Collaboration messaging-ops@contoso.example Network Director, Network network-ops@contoso.example Endpoints Director, EUC endpoint-ops@contoso.example Cloud platform Head of CCoE ccoe@contoso.example

docs.title: Email Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text:
KB-M365-021 · v1.6

Audience: Tier 1 agents

docs.title: Quick triage
docs.text:
Before changing settings, determine whether the issue is send, receive, calendar, or mobile-only.

  • Can the user sign in at outlook.office.com?
  • Does the issue affect one device or all devices?
  • Any recent password or MFA changes?
  • Is the mailbox full (quota warning)?

docs.title: Desktop Outlook
docs.text:
1. Repair Office apps from Settings → Apps
  2. Create a new Outlook profile if the profile is corrupt
  3. Clear Credential Manager entries for MicrosoftOffice*
  4. Check rules and Focused Inbox for missing mail

docs.title: Mobile mail
docs.text:
1. Remove and re-add the work account in Outlook mobile
  2. Confirm device compliance in Company Portal
  3. Do not recommend native iOS/Android mail for corporate accounts

docs.title: Escalate when
docs.text:
Messaging team

Mailbox corruption, litigation hold issues, transport rule failures, or hybrid mail flow problems — assign to Messaging with logs attached.

docs.title: Eligible hardware
docs.text: Item Refresh cycle Notes Laptop 3–4 years Role-based models only Monitor 5 years Max two per desk by default Headset As needed Noise-cancelling standard

docs.title: How to request
docs.text:
1. Open Service Request → Hardware
  2. Select catalog item and ship-to address
  3. Manager approval required for non-standard items
  4. Asset tag is recorded on delivery; return old kit on refresh

docs.title: Lost or stolen devices
docs.text:
Report immediately

Contact Service Desk and Security. Do not delay waiting on a replacement request.

docs.title: MFA recovery
docs.text:
Identity proofing required

Never remove MFA methods without verifying the user’s identity.

docs.title: Self-service
docs.text:
1. Use I can’t use my Microsoft Authenticator app on the sign-in page if available.
  2. Complete alternate proof (SMS/email) only if already registered.

docs.title: Agent procedure
docs.text:
1. Open the ticket and verify identity (employee ID, manager, corporate mobile last four / HR alternate).
  2. In the identity admin console, require re-registration of MFA methods.
  3. Temporary access pass (TAP) may be issued for a short window if policy allows.
  4. Stay on the line until the user enrolls a new method.
  5. Revoke TAP / temporary exceptions.
  6. Document methods removed/added.

docs.title: Escalate when
docs.text:
• Executive / VIP with no fallback methods → Identity Ops
  • Suspected account takeover → Security incident

docs.title: New Employee IT Onboarding Checklist
docs.text:
CHK-HRIT-003 · v3.0

For service desk agents and HR partners

docs.title: Before day one
docs.text:
• Create identity account from HR ticket (name, department, manager, start date)
  • Assign license bundle from role template
  • Stage laptop image (ship or pickup)
  • Add security groups (department + VPN if remote)
  • Schedule welcome email with first-login instructions

docs.title: Day one
docs.text:
• Verify MFA enrollment completed
  • Confirm email, Teams, and OneDrive sign-in
  • Map standard printers if site-based
  • Walk through password self-service registration
  • Share link to the knowledge base home

docs.title: Within first week
docs.text:
• Confirm access to role-specific applications
  • Close onboarding ticket only after user confirmation
  • Tag ticket with site and department for reporting

docs.title: Password Reset Procedure
docs.text:
SOP-AUTH-001 · v1.2

Audience: end users and Tier 1 agents

Prefer self-service. Agents use the procedure below only when self-service fails or the account is locked.

docs.title: Self-service steps
docs.text:
1. Go to password.contoso.example
  2. Enter your work email and complete the MFA challenge
  3. Choose a new password (14+ characters, mixed case, number, symbol)
  4. Sign out of all sessions, then sign back in

docs.title: Agent procedure
docs.text:
Verify identity first

Never reset a password without confirming employee ID, manager, and an approved contact method.

  1. Open the ticket and confirm identity (employee ID, manager, last four of corporate mobile or HR alternate)
  2. In the identity admin console, select Reset password and force change at next sign-in
  3. Clear temporary lockouts if present
  4. Provide the temporary password via a secure channel (never email permanent secrets)
  5. Advise MFA re-registration if prompts fail after reset
  6. Document steps and resolve the ticket

docs.title: Common issues
docs.text: Symptom What to try Password works, apps fail Clear cached credentials, restart, re-auth VPN/MFA Account locked Wait 15 minutes or clear lock in admin console MFA device lost Escalate to Identity with manager approval Shared/service account Do not reset without owner approval

docs.title: Supported devices
docs.text: Corporate networked printers, standard USB headsets, webcams, and docking stations listed in the hardware catalog.

docs.title: Printer issues checklist
docs.text:
1. Confirm the user is on the corporate network or VPN if required
  2. Reinstall the printer from the print server share
  3. Clear stuck jobs from the print queue
  4. Check toner/paper and physical error lights
  5. Escalate to Facilities for paper jams needing hardware service

docs.title: Docking and monitors
docs.text: Update dock firmware via Company Portal. Try a different USB‑C/Thunderbolt cable before replacing hardware.

docs.title: Remote Desktop Access SOP
docs.text:
SOP-EUC-011 · v1.0

Audience: support agents

docs.title: Policy
docs.text: Interactive remote control requires user consent, except break-glass admin scenarios approved by the duty manager.

docs.title: Starting a remote session
docs.text:
1. Open the remote support tool from the agent console
  2. Enter the ticket number (required for audit)
  3. Send the session invitation to the user
  4. Wait for Accept on the user device
  5. Announce actions before making system changes
  6. End the session and note the outcome in the ticket

docs.title: Prohibited actions
docs.text:
• Do not disable security tools without Security approval
  • Do not copy personal files off the device
  • Do not share your admin credentials with the user

docs.title: Request access
docs.text:
1. Portal → Collaboration → Shared mailbox access
  2. Provide mailbox SMTP and business justification
  3. Mailbox owner approves
  4. Messaging automation grants Full Access (and Send As if requested)

docs.title: SLA
docs.text: Action Target Standard grant 1 business day after approval Owner missing Return to requester within 4 business hours

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Check Mailbox missing in Outlook Restart Outlook; wait for directory sync (up to 60 minutes) Can read, cannot send Confirm Send As / Send on Behalf Mobile only Shared mailboxes are not always available in mobile native apps—use Outlook mobile

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Email troubleshooting
  • Collaboration service

docs.title: Software Installation Request Process
docs.text:
SOP-REQ-008 · v1.1

Only approved software may be installed on managed devices.

docs.title: Request flow
docs.text:
1. Search the software catalog for an existing package
  2. If listed → Request install (free tier tools auto-approve)
  3. If not listed → open a Service Request with:
    • Business justification
    • Cost center
    • Data classification
  4. Manager approves; Security reviews if regulated data is involved
  5. Packaging team publishes to Company Portal when approved

docs.title: SLA targets
docs.text: Request type Target Catalog install 8 business hours License add-on 2 business days New package (standard) 5 business days New package (security review) 10 business days

docs.title: VPN Access Guide
docs.text:
KB-NET-014 · v2.0

Audience: remote workers

Use the corporate VPN only when you need internal systems that are not published through the secure web gateway (legacy file shares, some finance apps, lab networks).

docs.title: Install and connect
docs.text:
1. Install Contoso Secure Access from Company Portal / Managed Software Center
  2. Sign in with your work account (SSO)
  3. Approve the MFA prompt
  4. Confirm status shows Connected and an internal IP is assigned

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Collect logs first

Use Help → Export logs before contacting the service desk.

  • Home Wi‑Fi blocking UDP — try alternate port profile or a wired connection
  • Certificate errors — restart the device; ensure date/time is automatic
  • Split tunnel — internet should still work; only corporate routes use the VPN

docs.title: Security rules
docs.text:
• Do not share VPN profiles
  • Personal devices require MDM enrollment before VPN is allowed

docs.title: Capacity
docs.text:
Platform teams review utilization monthly for:

  • Identity authentication peak RPS
  • VPN concurrent sessions
  • Mailbox storage growth
  • Azure subscription quotas

Service Desk escalates “slow but up” patterns that may indicate capacity strain.

docs.title: Continuity
docs.text: Service tier Recovery objective (sample) T0 RTO 4h / RPO 15m T1 RTO 24h / RPO 4h T2 Best effort

docs.title: What agents should do
docs.text:
• Tag outages with region and service
  • Avoid ad-hoc workarounds that break audit trails
  • Link major incident records when declared

docs.title: When to declare
docs.text:
• Sev 1 impact, or
  • Widespread Sev 2 with unclear ETA, or
  • Executive / regulatory visibility expected

docs.title: Roles
docs.text: Role Duty Incident commander Runs the bridge, sets cadence Technical lead Drives mitigation Communications lead User and stakeholder updates Scribe Timeline for post-incident review

docs.title: Cadence
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Detect
  Detect --> Declare
  Declare --> Mitigate
  Mitigate --> Communicate
  Communicate --> Mitigate: still open
  Mitigate --> Resolve
  Resolve --> Review
  Review --> [*]

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Incident severity
  • Escalation matrix

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Changes to production IT services are planned, reviewed, and communicated to reduce unplanned downtime.

docs.title: Change types
docs.text: Type Approval Lead time Standard Pre-approved As scheduled Normal CAB / manager 3 business days Emergency Duty manager As needed

docs.title: Service desk role
docs.text:
• Communicate maintenance windows to users
  • Monitor incidents during change implementation
  • Link related incidents to the change record

docs.title: Incident Severity Levels
docs.text:
POL-IM-002 · v1.4

Severity is based on business impact, not preference alone.

docs.title: Severity table
docs.text: Severity Definition Initial response Example Sev 1 Critical service down for many users or revenue impact 15 minutes Email outage company-wide Sev 2 Major function degraded; limited workaround 1 hour VPN down for one region Sev 3 Single user or small group; business continues 4 hours One laptop cannot print Sev 4 Low impact / cosmetic 1 business day Desktop shortcut missing

docs.title: How to choose severity
docs.text:
• Count of users affected
  • Whether a viable workaround exists
  • Regulatory, safety, or security implications
  • Production vs. test environment

docs.title: Communication
docs.text: Sev 1 and Sev 2 require status updates on the major-incident channel until resolved or mitigated.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This SLA covers Contoso IT services delivered through the Service Desk for full-time employees and long-term contractors.

  • Incident response and resolution targets by severity
  • Service request fulfillment targets
  • Planned maintenance windows

docs.title: Availability targets
docs.text:
Core collaboration services target 99.9% monthly availability excluding announced maintenance.

Service Target availability Email / Teams 99.9% VPN / remote access 99.5% Service desk portal 99.5% Identity / SSO 99.95%

docs.title: Exclusions
docs.text: Force majeure, customer-owned networks, and unsanctioned software are excluded from SLA credits.

docs.title: When to escalate
docs.text:
Escalate when the SLA is at risk, the issue exceeds Tier 1 skills, or a major incident is declared.

Level Owner Trigger Tier 1 Service Desk Initial triage and known fixes Tier 2 Platform teams Complex config, multi-user impact Tier 3 Engineering / vendor Code defects, infrastructure faults Major Incident Duty manager Sev 1 or widespread Sev 2

docs.title: Contacts
docs.text:
• Duty manager on-call — PagerDuty schedule IT-Duty
  • Security CSIRT — security@contoso.example
  • Vendor bridge — open only after Tier 3 acknowledges

docs.title: Documentation required
docs.text: Every escalation must include timeline, impact statement, reproduction steps, and current workaround.

docs.title: Knowledge Base Authoring Guidelines
docs.text:
STD-KB-004 · v1.0

Consistent structure improves search, navigation, and handoffs between agents.

docs.title: Why structure matters
docs.text: Articles with clear titles, short procedures, and consistent headings are easier to find and faster to follow under pressure.

docs.title: Article template
docs.text:
1. Title — task-oriented (e.g. Reset your VPN client)
  2. Summary — 1–2 sentences
  3. Before you begin — prerequisites
  4. Steps — numbered, one action each
  5. Result — what success looks like
  6. Related articles — links

docs.title: Style rules
docs.text:
• Active voice, second person (you)
  • Screenshots only when the UI is non-obvious
  • Call out risk with warning or danger notes
  • Review quarterly or after major product changes

docs.title: Document structure
docs.text:
• Use clear section headings for every major topic
  • Prefer numbered steps for procedures and bullets for options
  • Group content by audience need: how-to, policy, or reference

docs.title: Security Incident Quick Reference
docs.text:
REF-SEC-002 · Internal

Treat suspected security events as high priority.

docs.title: If you suspect a security incident
docs.text:
Examples: phishing click, ransomware symptoms, lost device with data, unexpected admin prompts, accidental external data email.

  1. Prefer preserving evidence — disconnect network if malware is active; do not wipe unless instructed
  2. Contact Service Desk and mark the ticket Security
  3. Notify Security CSIRT for confirmed or high-confidence events
  4. Do not delete emails or files that may be evidence

docs.title: Phishing reports
docs.text:
• Use the Report phishing button in Outlook
  • Forward as attachment only if the button is missing
  • Never enter credentials on linked pages from suspicious mail

docs.title: Service desk first response
docs.text: If credential theft is likely: reset password, revoke sessions, review MFA methods, then escalate to CSIRT with a timeline.

docs.title: Service catalog overview
docs.text: The service catalog is the menu of IT offerings Contoso provides to the business. Each offering has a clear outcome, request path, and owner.

docs.title: Domains
docs.text: Domain Examples Entry point Identity and access Accounts, MFA, groups, SSO Portal → Access End-user computing Laptops, peripherals, imaging Portal → Hardware Collaboration Email, Teams, SharePoint Portal → Collaboration Network and remote access VPN, Wi-Fi, DNS Portal → Network Cloud platforms Azure landing zones, subscriptions Portal → Cloud

docs.title: Request classes
docs.text: Class Description Typical SLA Incident Something is broken Severity-based Service request Something is needed Fulfillment target Access request Entitlement change 1–2 business days Standard change Pre-approved change As scheduled

docs.title: Lifecycle
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  D[Demand] --> C[Catalog item]
  C --> R[Request]
  R --> A[Approve]
  A --> F[Fulfill]
  F --> M[Measure]
  M --> C

Owners review catalog items quarterly for cost, risk, and demand.

docs.title: Includes

  • Landing zone connectivity (hub/spoke)
  • Platform policy / blueprints
  • Shared logging and security baselines

docs.title: Request path
docs.text: Portal → Cloud → New subscription (manager + cloud finance approval)

Elevated volume

Do not share break-glass credentials

Emergency admin accounts require dual control and must be checked back into the vault within 60 minutes.

Deep dive — why this ticket spiked

A mobile OS update invalidated push MFA tokens for a subset of Android devices. Workaround: fall back to TOTP, then re-register the authenticator app.

docs.title: Multi-path procedures (tabs)
docs.text:
End userTier 1 agentAutomation
  1. Open password.contoso.example
  2. Complete MFA
  3. Set a 14+ character password
  4. Sign out of all apps and back in
  1. Verify identity (employee ID + manager + approved contact)
  2. Admin reset with force change at next sign-in
  3. Deliver temporary secret on a secure channel
  4. Document steps; resolve ticket
automation-hook.yml
trigger: ticket.category == "password-reset"
actions:
  - verify_identity:
      methods: [employee_id, manager_ok, mfa_device]
  - idp.reset_password:
      force_change: true
  - notify:
      channel: secure_message

docs.title: Annotated automation
Interactive-style data
docs.text:

docs.title: Intake & escalation
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  U[User] --> P[Portal / Chat / Phone]
  P --> T1[Tier 1]
  T1 -->|Known fix| R[Resolve + KB]
  T1 -->|Complex| T2[Tier 2]
  T2 --> T3[Tier 3 / Vendor]
  T1 -->|Sev 1| MI[Major incident]
  T2 --> MI
  MI --> R

docs.title: Agent assist sequence
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  actor Agent
  participant KB as Knowledge base
  participant SM as Service mgmt
  participant User

  User->>SM: Open ticket
  Agent->>KB: Search symptom
  alt Article found
    KB-->>Agent: Procedure hit
    Agent->>User: Guided resolution
  else No article
    Agent->>SM: Escalate + flag KB gap
  end

docs.title: CMDB sketch
docs.text:
erDiagram
  PERSON ||--o{ DEVICE : assigned
  PERSON ||--o{ TICKET : reports
  DEVICE }o--|| SITE : located-at
  TICKET }o--|| SERVICE : affects
  SERVICE ||--o{ SLA_TARGET : measured-by

docs.title: Journey — new hire day one
docs.text:
journey
  title New hire IT day one
  section Arrive
    Collect laptop: 5: HR, Desk
    First sign-in: 4: Employee
  section Secure
    Enroll MFA: 5: Employee
    VPN test: 3: Employee
  section Validate
    Apps open: 4: Employee, Manager
    Ticket closed: 5: Desk

docs.title: Command & config samples
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml (excerpt)PowerShell deployGitHub Actions idea 
theme:
  name: material
  features:
    - navigation.tabs
    - navigation.instant
    - search.suggest
    - content.code.annotate
    - content.tabs.link
markdown_extensions:
  - admonition
  - pymdownx.tabbed:
      alternate_style: true
  - pymdownx.superfences:
      custom_fences:
        - name: mermaid
          class: mermaid
          format: !!python/name:pymdownx.superfences.fence_code_format

.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
mkdocs build
mkdocs gh-deploy --force

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: mkdocs gh-deploy --force

docs.title: Cards, buttons, and iconography
docs.text:
• Ship docs like product

    Versioned Markdown, PR review, instant preview, static publish

  • Search-first UX

    Suggestions, highlighting, shareable deep links into hits

  • Theme aware

    Light / dark / system — diagrams and chrome follow the palette

  • Visual systems thinking

    Mermaid flows beat five-page prose for escalations

docs.title: Checklists and definitions
docs.text:
• Sticky navigation tabs
  • Instant loading and search suggest
  • Custom header action (star → this page)
  • Mermaid and annotated code
Incident Unplanned service interruption or degradation. Request Planned fulfillment work (access, hardware, software). Problem Underlying cause tracked across related incidents.

docs.title: Math and footnotes
docs.text:
Availability:

\[ A = \frac{\text{uptime}}{\text{uptime} + \text{downtime}} \]

Inline form \(A \ge 0.999\) for core collaboration services1.

  1. See SLA overview for exclusions and maintenance windows. ↩
